title: 7-day motor imagery BCI EEG dataset from Zhou et al 2021
description: A 7-day longitudinal motor imagery brain-computer interface (BCI) EEG dataset comprising 20 healthy participants performing four imagery tasks (left hand, right hand, both feet, and rest) across 7 sessions. The dataset contains 33,600 trials recorded at 500 Hz from 41 EEG channels using a Neuroscan SynAmps2 system with visual arrow cue stimulation. Data were preprocessed and annotated with HED 8.4.0 tags, supporting BCI research and algorithm development for motor imagery decoding.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 500 Hz sampling rate from 41 channels using a Neuroscan SynAmps2 system with Ag/AgCl sensors referenced to vertex (Cz) and grounded at AFz. Online bandpass filtering (0.5-100 Hz) and 50 Hz notch filtering were applied. Participants performed four motor imagery tasks (left hand, right hand, feet, rest) cued by visual arrows in a synchronous, offline paradigm without feedback. Each session consisted of 6 runs with 40 trials per run, yielding 240 trials per session across 7 sessions per subject.
